basics.description:
![Abstract Security](https://cybersecurity-excellence-awards.com/wp-content/uploads/163661.png)

# ABSTRACT SECURITY
**The Security Data Pipeline Platform.** Deliver enriched, normalized security events to any destination — in real time, at scale.

[abstract.security](https://abstract.security) · [Documentation](https://docs.abstractsecurity.app/docs/integrations/destination-integrations/azure-eventhub-destination/) · [Book a demo](https://abstract.security)

---
### Azure EventHub — Destination Integration
Abstract **writes to** Event Hub. This guided deployment provisions the namespace, the destination hub, and a least-privilege **Send** SAS rule (keys are never exposed in outputs), with optional Entra ID delivery and Safe Mode networking so Abstract can always reach it.

✓ Standard tier minimum  ✓ Least-privilege Send  ✓ Optional Entra ID (RBAC) delivery

When the deployment finishes, the **Outputs** tab gives you the `EventHub Name` and tells you exactly where to copy the Send connection string for the Abstract destination modal.

basics.name: namespaceName
basics.label: Event Hubs namespace name
basics.toolTip: Globally unique, 6-50 characters, letters/numbers/hyphens, starts with a letter. Becomes <name>.servicebus.windows.net.

constraints.validationMessage: 6-50 chars, letters/numbers/hyphens, must start with a letter and end with a letter or number.

basics.name: sku
basics.label: Pricing tier
basics.toolTip: Abstract requires Standard as the minimum tier. Premium uses Processing Units and dedicated capacity.

basics.name: capacity
basics.label: Capacity (Throughput / Processing Units)
basics.toolTip: ~1 TU per 1 MB/s of expected delivery throughput. Premium accepts 1, 2, 4, 8 or 16 PUs only.

basics.name: autoInflate
basics.label: Enable Auto-Inflate (Standard tier scales Throughput Units automatically)

options.text: Abstract writes processed events into this single hub. Its name goes in the 'EventHub Name' field of the Abstract destination integration. Abstract docs recommend at least 4 partitions.

elements.name: eventHubName
elements.label: Event Hub name
elements.toolTip: Enter this value in the 'EventHub Name' field of the Abstract destination integration.

constraints.validationMessage: 1-256 chars: letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, underscores; starts/ends alphanumeric.

elements.name: partitionCount
elements.label: Partitions
elements.toolTip: Abstract docs recommend at least 4. Partitions cannot be reduced after creation.

elements.name: retentionDays
elements.label: Message retention (days)
elements.toolTip: Size to ride out downstream consumer downtime.

options.text: The Abstract EventHub Destination uses a Send connection string. A least-privilege Send-only SAS rule is always created (its keys are NEVER shown in deployment outputs). Optionally also grant a service principal the Azure Event Hubs Data Sender role for Entra ID based delivery.

elements.name: sendRuleName
elements.label: Send SAS rule name
elements.toolTip: Least-privilege Send shared access policy. Use its primary connection string in the Abstract destination modal instead of RootManageSharedAccessKey.

constraints.validationMessage: 1-50 chars: letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, underscores.

elements.name: enableRbac
elements.label: Also grant a service principal the Azure Event Hubs Data Sender role (Entra ID delivery, no shared keys)

elements.name: principalId
elements.label: Service principal object ID
elements.toolTip: Object ID of the Enterprise Application (service principal) Abstract authenticates as - NOT the Application (client) ID.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a GUID (the service principal OBJECT id).

elements.name: roleDefinitionName
elements.label: Event Hubs role
elements.defaultValue: Azure Event Hubs Data Sender (least privilege)

allowedValues.label: Azure Event Hubs Data Sender (least privilege)

elements.name: profile
elements.label: Security profile
elements.defaultValue: Safe Mode — public access stays on (recommended for onboarding)
elements.toolTip: Safe Mode guarantees Abstract can reach the namespace to deliver events. Harden later by redeploying with IP Allowlist, Private Only or Hybrid.

allowedValues.label: Safe Mode — public access stays on (recommended for onboarding)
IP Allowlist — public, firewall default-deny with allowed CIDRs
Private Only — no public access, Private Endpoint required
Hybrid — Private Endpoint + public IP allowlist

options.text: Private Only blocks ALL public access. Abstract (external SaaS) must then reach the namespace over your private connectivity, or event delivery will fail. Verify this before choosing it.

elements.name: ipCsv
elements.label: Allowed public IP ranges (comma-separated CIDRs)
elements.toolTip: Abstract egress IPs plus your admin ranges.

constraints.validationMessage: Comma-separated IPv4 addresses or CIDR ranges.

elements.name: subnetId
elements.label: Subnet resource ID for the Private Endpoint
elements.toolTip: Full ARM resource ID of an existing subnet.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a full subnet resource ID.
Must be the resource ID of a privatelink.servicebus.windows.net Private DNS zone.

elements.name: allowAzureServices
elements.label: Allow trusted Microsoft services through the firewall
